clip_issues.description: A plastic bottle appears next to the newspaper on the floor, which was not present in Clip 1.
clip_issues.fix: Remove bottle from prompt or inpaint erase.

clip_issues.description: The Detective's facial features and hair change significantly, making him look like a different actor. The folio turns into a bound paper book.
clip_issues.fix: Use consistent character ref image. Specify 'leather folio' not 'book'.

clip_issues.description: The item the Detective is holding changes from an open paper book to a closed brown leather document case held under his arm.
clip_issues.fix: Ensure same folio across all clips.

transition_issues.description: Train exterior design changes; red M logo from Clip 1 disappears in Clip 2.
transition_issues.fix_suggestion: Use last frame of Clip 1 as image prompt for Clip 2.

transition_issues.description: Black folio handed to Detective in Clip 2 instantly becomes open paper book in Clip 3. Woman walking back to board train is now standing still.
transition_issues.fix_suggestion: Re-prompt Clip 3 with black folio, not book. Ensure Woman's action matches.

transition_issues.description: Abrupt change in character pose and prop state. Detective goes from staring at open book to standing casually with closed brown folder under his arm.
transition_issues.fix_suggestion: Bridge by having Detective close folio at start of Clip 4, or ensure he's still holding it with both hands as camera pulls back.

cross_film_consistency.notes: 180° rule perfectly maintained. However, severe prop morphing (folio → book → brown bag) and facial inconsistencies break continuity.

fixes_vs_v2: doors stay open through clip end (R11 action completion worked)
drone pullback now executes (R8/directing beats worked)
no extreme folio morphing within single clip (R14 inoculation worked)
new_issues_vs_v2: cross-clip prop drift (folio → book → brown bag) — caused by parallel-only generation without tail-frame chaining between c01-c02-c03-c04
_regression_notes.validates_rule: R15 (chain vs parallel) — the new issues would be prevented by running c01 parallel then c02/c03/c04 serially with extract-frame chaining
